summary: Completed S7 PR-15 hard cutover: global player now supports persisted audio effects (silence trim, volume boost + compressor, mono mix) through a lazy Web Audio graph with CORS-safe fallback and full component acceptance coverage.
how_to_test:
cd apps/web && npm test -- "src/__tests__/components/GlobalPlayerAudioEffects.test.tsx"
cd apps/web && npm test -- "src/__tests__/components/GlobalPlayerFooter.test.tsx" "src/__tests__/components/GlobalPlayerMediaSession.test.tsx" "src/__tests__/components/GlobalPlayerPersistence.test.tsx" "src/__tests__/components/GlobalPlayerQueue.test.tsx" "src/__tests__/components/GlobalPlayerAudioEffects.test.tsx"
cd apps/web && npm run typecheck  # currently fails on pre-existing unrelated errors
risks: Silence-trim uses fixed defaults (-45dB threshold, 400ms window), which may under-trim noisy podcasts.
When media source CORS blocks MediaElementAudioSourceNode creation, effects are intentionally disabled and playback falls back to plain <audio>.
